name: Palmyra Bel
description: The Temple of Bel, located in Palmyra, Syria, was a monumental religious structure dedicated to the Mesopotamian god Bel, who was syncretized with the local Palmyrene deity Baalshamin. It served as a central place of worship in the city, reflecting Palmyra's unique blend of Greco-Roman and Mesopotamian cultural influences. The temple complex, a vast courtyard enclosed by colonnades, housed the main sanctuary and various chapels dedicated to other deities, including Yarhibol and Aglibol. Its construction began in the 1st century AD and continued over several decades, showcasing intricate architectural details and religious symbolism. The Temple of Bel was not merely a place of worship but also a symbol of Palmyra's power, prosperity, and religious syncretism. It played a crucial role in the city's cultural and religious life, hosting important ceremonies and festivals that celebrated the divine presence of Bel and other associated deities. The temple's destruction by ISIS in 2015 was a tragic loss of cultural heritage, representing an attack on the rich history and religious traditions of Palmyra and the broader Syrian region. The temple's ruins still stand as a testament to the enduring legacy of Palmyrene civilization and its complex religious beliefs, where Bel was considered the supreme deity, responsible for order, fertility, and protection. The temple's architecture, combining Roman, Greek, and Mesopotamian elements, embodies the cultural fusion that defined Palmyra. The site held immense spiritual and historical significance for the people of Palmyra and continues to be remembered as a symbol of their identity and heritage. Its destruction caused global outrage and highlighted the importance of preserving cultural heritage in conflict zones.
subtitle: A center for worship and culture.

keyMyths.title: Bel's Supremacy
keyMyths.description: Bel, as a syncretized deity, was considered the supreme god of Palmyra, embodying power, fertility, and protection. Worshippers believed that offering sacrifices and prayers to Bel would ensure prosperity and divine favor.

keyMyths.title: The Temple's Construction
keyMyths.description: The Temple of Bel was built over several decades, reflecting Palmyra's growing wealth and influence. Each addition and alteration to the temple complex was believed to enhance Bel's divine presence and strengthen the city's connection to the gods.

sources.author: Warwick Ball
sources.work: Rome in the East: The Transformation of an Empire
sources.description: Provides historical context for Palmyra's Roman influence.
sources.text: Provides historical context for Palmyra's Roman influence.
